# Skills & Agents Index (auto-generated)

Last regenerated: 2026-05-21

> This file is generated by `plugins/forge/scripts/generate-index.sh`. Do not edit manually.
> To regenerate: `plugins/forge/scripts/generate-index.sh`

## Skills (slash commands)

### debugging

- `/diagnose-deep` — Rigorous four-phase debugging workflow for bugs that resist quick fixes. Invoked by forge:diagnose when a defect needs root-cause discipline rather than a guess-and-check loop.
- `/tdd` — Rigid red-green-refactor discipline for any feature, bug fix, or behavior change. Invoked by forge:execute-ticket and forge:diagnose-deep when production code is about to be written.

### flutter + project init

- `/dart-collect-coverage` — Collect Dart/Flutter test coverage with the `coverage` package and produce an LCOV report. Use when a Dart or Flutter project needs a coverage run, an LCOV artifact, or branch/function-level metrics.
- `/dart-fix-runtime-errors` — Resolve Dart static-analysis errors (type system, null safety, override soundness) using `dart analyze` + `dart fix`, then verify via `dart test`. Use when the analyzer reports diagnostics or when CI fails on a type / null-safety / override issue.
- `/flutter-fix-layout-issues` — Resolve Flutter layout constraint violations — RenderFlex overflow, unbounded viewport/width, ParentData misuse — using a diagnostic-to-fix lookup. Use when a Flutter app throws a layout exception, shows the yellow/black overflow stripes, or surfaces the red error screen.
- `/project-init` — Bootstrap a new project's Claude Code setup — stack interview, CLAUDE.md from template, kit-* skills copied from `plugins/forge/skill-templates/<stack>/`, settings.json, tracker, optional Linear project. `--tracker-only` re-runs only the tracker setup on an existing project.

### meta

- `/caveman` — Switch to ultra-compressed communication mode that drops filler while preserving full technical accuracy.
- `/handoff` — Compact the current conversation into a handoff document for another agent to pick up.
- `/hello` — Smoke-test skill for plugin install verification. Use when the user types /hello to confirm the forge plugin is installed and reachable.
- `/writing-skill` — Author or refactor a forge plugin skill so it conforms to plugin-wide policy. Use when creating a new SKILL.md, splitting an oversized skill, migrating a legacy command into the plugin, or verifying a skill before opening a PR.

### planning

- `/brainstorm` — Stress-test a feature or design via guided dialectic before any implementation begins.
- `/diagnose` — Disciplined 6-phase diagnosis loop for bugs and performance regressions — builds a fast feedback loop first, then reproduces, hypothesises, instruments, fixes, and regresses-tests.
- `/grill-me` — Interview the user relentlessly about a plan or design until reaching shared understanding, resolving each branch of the decision tree one question at a time.
- `/grill-with-docs` — Grilling session that challenges a plan against the project's domain model, sharpens terminology, and updates CONTEXT.md and ADRs inline as decisions crystallise.
- `/prototype` — Build a throwaway prototype to answer a design question before committing — routes to a terminal logic app or multiple UI variations depending on the question being asked.
- `/zoom-out` — Map the relevant modules and callers around unfamiliar code, using the project's domain vocabulary and surfacing any ADRs that constrain the area.

### review

- `/graph-refresh` — Thin wrapper around `code-review-graph build --incremental`. Rebuilds the code-review graph for the current project and reports duration and node/edge count diff.
- `/handle-review-feedback` — Process PR or Linear code-review feedback by classifying each item as valid, needs-clarification, or push-back before implementing anything.
- `/review` — Local 3-agent code review focused on architecture, security, and testing. Read-only — emits structured findings for the EPIC B classifier (no auto-fix).
- `/simplify` — Review the most recent code change for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix every issue found.
- `/simplify-branch` — Run reuse, quality, and efficiency review across all commits in the current branch versus its base, then fix every issue found.

### tracker pipeline

- `/commit` — Create a git commit linked to a tracker ticket via magic words; dispatches to the configured backend (linear/github/markdown) via tracker.json, with fallback to legacy Linear prefix detection.
- `/create-epic` — Draft a tracker epic plus its sub-issues from a chat brief; interview, propose structure, then create on confirmation with dependency links.
- `/create-ticket` — Create a single tracker ticket from a chat brief or `docs/0X_*.md` plan; routes between three modes — A (doc-backed), B (inline, four flavours), or M (manual-setup).
- `/epic-close` — Close a tracker epic via 3-option decision tree (merge / draft PR / cleanup-only). Tests-pass hard gate + simplify + docs sync + DRY comment, with the path-specific follow-ups dispatched after the user picks. Does NOT change ticket statuses or rewrite epic descriptions.
- `/execute-epic` — Orchestrate execution of a tracker epic — branch setup, manual-setup gate, dependency-ordered story execution via subagents, per-ticket commits, aggregated manual test cases on the epic.
- `/execute-ticket` — Execute a single tracker ticket — read it, plan, implement using project `/kit-*` skills if any, run linter + tests, generate manual test cases, post comment.
- `/pr-create` — Create a draft PR for a tracker epic — reads the epic + branch, generates an epic-linked PR body from sub-issues and commits, opens the PR via gh CLI.

## Agents (Task subagents)

### runner agents

- `linter-runner` — Universal linter executor. Reads project's CLAUDE.md > Essential commands > Lint (or Format), runs the linter (optionally a subset), parses output, and reports errors/warnings. In `mode=fix`, applies auto-fixes (`--fix` flag, `dart fix --apply`, etc.) and re-runs. Use after any code change, before committing, or when invoked from `/execute-ticket` and `/execute-epic`.
- `test-runner` — Universal test executor. Reads project's CLAUDE.md > Essential commands > Test, runs tests (optionally a subset), parses output, and reports pass/fail with structured failures. In `mode=fix`, attempts to fix failing tests by analyzing stack traces and applying minimal edits, looping up to 3 times. Use after any code change, or when invoked from `/execute-ticket` and `/execute-epic`.
